externalAdoptionEvidenceProof.summary: External adoption evidence is structurally valid but stale for the release manifest.

finalStableV4ClaimPacket.copyReadyClaim: ShipGuard source-class fixture coverage is promoted; stable-v4 publication is still blocked until real evidence passes.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the review-scope mapping actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a review-existing-fixture action for the promoted public fixture
Markdown renders External Evidence Review Scope Mapping with broad-review and unmapped-output rejects
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

items.proofBoundary: Real independent adoption evidence must pass before stable-v4 claims are allowed; GitHub stars, downloads, generated starter files, vague testimonials, and maintainer-only private app runs do not count.

items.proofBoundary: Real final security-review evidence must pass before stable-v4 claims are allowed; vague self-review notes, missing methodology, missing reviewed surfaces, private-data leaks, or open critical/high findings do not count.
items.summary: Vague or incomplete security-review evidence does not satisfy final security proof.

nonClaims: This checklist does not prove stable v4 by itself.
Every listed item must pass from real publication evidence before stable-v4 claims are allowed.
Do not skip later blockers merely because an earlier gate is still failing.

firstBlockingGate.summary: Independent adoption evidence is stale for this release manifest.

nonClaims: This packet does not publish a GitHub release.
This packet does not prove OpenAI marketplace acceptance.
Fixture freshness records prove tooling only; they do not prove real stable-v4 publication.
Structurally valid external records are rejected when generatedAt predates the release manifest.
proofOrder: Run LaunchKey release-candidate proof with package install, upgrade, rollback, release assets, adoption, and security receipts.
Publish the GitHub release with stable-v4 release notes and required release-proof assets.
Run stable-publication against the published release metadata, downloaded assets, independent adoption evidence, and final security review evidence.
Use a passing stable-publication report as the only local permission to claim ShipGuard v4 is stable.

requiredEvidence.summary: GitHub release metadata was loaded and required release assets are present.
Release notes explicitly describe stable-v4 publication proof.

launchKeyCandidateDiagnostics.nestedBlockingSummary: LaunchKey package install, upgrade, and rollback proof passed.

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: A release tarball installs into a clean prefix, reports the expected version through both CLI aliases, runs validation, and leaves no generated/cache/VCS/package sidecar files in the installed tree.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Fresh package install

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: A previous release package installs first, the candidate package upgrades the same prefix, both aliases report the candidate version, validation passes, and the upgraded tree is clean.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Same-prefix upgrade

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: A temporary candidate install can be removed without leaving ShipGuard package state behind.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Rollback cleanup

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: LaunchKey may download public GitHub release assets for candidate readiness, but stable-publication verifies downloaded or supplied release assets again.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: GitHub release asset download

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: Downloaded or supplied release assets can be consumed by release-consume verification; stable-publication still owns the final consumer gate.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Release-consume proof

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: LaunchKey can validate adoption-evidence structure, but real independent adoption for stable v4 is proven by the stable-publication adoption gate.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Independent adoption evidence

requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.requirement: LaunchKey can validate security-evidence structure and scope, but final stable-v4 security proof is proven by the stable-publication security gate.
requiredLaunchKeyProofAreas.title: Final security review evidence

adoptionEvidenceChecklist.passDecision: Pass only when a non-maintainer actor supplies redacted/public command and artifact evidence for real ShipGuard use.

evidenceLadder.description: Install and verify ShipGuard from public release assets only; this proves public consumability, not adoption.
Private maintainer app runs may expose ShipGuard product gaps, but they do not satisfy independent adoption evidence.
A non-maintainer user, repo, issue, PR, or redacted external install report must supply the adoption record.
A security review record must cover the required ShipGuard surfaces and show no open critical or high findings.

files.purpose: Human checklist for collecting the real stable-v4 publication packet.
Machine-readable draft checklist with the seven required stable-publication gates.
Draft-only independent adoption evidence record. Fill with real external evidence before use.
Draft-only final security-review evidence record. Fill with real review evidence before use.

instructions: These files are generated as a starter kit only; unchanged starter-kit JSON must not pass stable-publication.
Replace placeholder values with real reviewed evidence, keep private paths and private app details redacted, then pass the completed files back to v4 stable-publication.
Use the report's firstBlockingGate and this checklist together; do not claim stable v4 until v4 stable-publication returns pass.

securityReviewChecklist.passDecision: Pass only when every required surface is reviewed, methodology and findings summary are attached, non-claims are explicit, and criticalOpen plus highOpen are both 0.

instructions: Copy a template into a private evidence directory, replace placeholder text with real reviewed evidence, and keep private app details redacted.
Do not use unchanged templates as proof. They intentionally default to draft/privateDataRedacted=false so stable-publication blocks fake evidence.
Pass the completed JSON file or directory to --external-adoption-evidence or --security-review-evidence.

failCriteria: The unchanged template or generated starter file is submitted as evidence.
Evidence relies only on GitHub downloads or maintainer-only runs.
The record contains local paths, private app identifiers, screenshots with private data, tokens, or account data.
passCriteria: At least one JSON record has status=pass.
commands, artifacts, outcome, and nonClaims are present.
The record is public-shareable or summary-shareable with consent/privacy reviewed.
templates.stableRequirement: Real independent public or private-redacted external adoption evidence; unchanged template records are draft-only and must not pass.
templates.title: Independent adoption evidence

failCriteria: The unchanged template or generated starter file is submitted as evidence.

scope covers cli, plugin, github-actions, release-proof, package-install, and redaction-privacy.
methodology, commands, artifacts, findingsSummary, and nonClaims are present.

templates.title: Final security review evidence

Security proof requires reviewed stable-v4 surfaces, methodology, redacted artifacts, findings summary, and no open critical/high findings.
